text: 2 The world faces multiple water challenges; pollution, water-related ecosystems degradation, 3 overextraction, ﬂooding and inequitable access. Insuﬃcient funding, ineﬀective implementation and, 4 in part, a misdiagnosis of the causes have contributed to inadequate progress and prioritized some 5 responses (e.g. water infrastructure) over others (e.g. water reallocation). We rethink the possible 6 responses to the world’s water crises with a Beyond Growth framing and compare it to mainstream 7 thinking. Beyond Growth, systems thinking informed by justice principles and relationality, prioritizes 8 the most disadvantaged and seeks to decouple economic growth from environmental degradation by 9 overcoming policy capture and inertia and fostering justice-principled institutional changes. 10 11 By 12 R.
